sprint: Retro Guardrails Audit — the Whole Artwork Presentation Group Now Honours "editable" Too
task: A direct follow-up to a standing instruction -- apply the three governing rules going forward AND in retro. Auditing the prior Frame-editable fix against Rule 2/3 found a second real gap: the Artwork Presentation subgroup (Frame Variation swatches, Presentation/Lighting/Caption/Paper/Mat dropdowns, all built by js/workspaceBuilder.js's CONTROL_CATALOG) and the paper/mat controls inside Frame Style had NO editable gating at all -- a Story Author could still change these on a Place its Builder author marked non-editable. Presented two scope options; user chose to lock the whole group together ("if a Theme Author says don't let them change this Place's look, nothing about how the picture is framed, lit, captioned, or matted can be touched"). Fixed with one shared _isCtxLocked(ctx) helper wired into _buildSelectRow/_buildSwatchRow/_buildToggleRow's own __sync functions (ctx.getPlaceEditable, a live function reference, mirroring the existing ctx.getPlaceId convention -- omitted by every non-Place caller, so Layout/Text/Sticker controls are unaffected); js/cardDesigner.js's _refreshFrameWorkspace() now passes getPlaceEditable:_currentPlaceEditable into both the 'frame' and 'holder.image' panel contexts, and the Artwork Presentation subgroup gained its own lock note sharing Frame Look's class. New CSS gives disabled swatches/dropdowns a visible locked state. Verified via two new Playwright variants (locked vs. open) confirming every Frame Look button, Frame Style slider, Artwork Presentation dropdown, and Frame Variation swatch locks/unlocks together correctly, plus full regression across the existing 31-script suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged, zero console errors.
